key_insight: A combined CEL simulation and ANN framework can accurately predict and optimize friction stir welding quality in HDPE by linking process settings to temperature, force, and defect formation.
engineering_relevance: The study provides practical welding windows that improve joint robustness, showing that bottom preheating and higher rotational speed can reduce force fluctuations and defect risk in thermoplastic FSW.
method: 3D coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model for thermal-mechanical welding behavior
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental responses for temperature and axial force prediction
Experimental validation across rotational speed, welding speed, and bottom preheating conditions
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved strong predictive performance with R2 values between 0.91 and 0.99, and the CEL model matched observed void formation and thermal-mechanical responses well.
watch_item: The process map is based on a limited experimental parameter range, so predictions may not generalize reliably outside the tested speeds and preheating conditions.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
